2606.12387.title: TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
2606.12387.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have democratized database access through Text-to-SQL, but moving from prototypes to production remains difficult. Real deployments must handle strict SQL dialects, massive schemas, and evolving user preferences, while supervised fine-tuning is costly and rigid and agentic test-time scaling is expensive. We present Tahoe, a system that treats prompt optimization as a dynamic data management problem. Tahoe uses an error-driven hint learning pipeline across Development and Deployment to consolidate debugging traces into a structured Hint Bank. Compiler feedback is distilled into reusable Syntax Hints for dialect-specific rules, while execution and user feedback are converted into Semantic Hints for schema- and user-specific logic. Tahoe further introduces a Strategy Layer that models conflicting user intents as competing strategies under shared natural-language triggers, with recency signals and post-learning attribution statistics that summarize empirical success, harm, inertness, and support. At inference time, Tahoe retrieves relevant hints and guides the LLM through Logic Planning followed by SQL Synthesis. We implement and evaluate the development-phase workflow, leaving deployment-time human-feedback updates for future work. On Spider 2.0-Snow, Tahoe substantially improves Text-to-SQL without updating model parameters. On 113 supervised Spider 2.0-Snow-0212 examples using GPT-5.5, Tahoe raises pass rate from 61.95 percent to 79.42 percent and pass-at-4 from 72.57 percent to 87.61 percent, achieves 100 percent Snowflake syntax pass rate, and reduces average compiler-feedback critic rounds from 2.79 to 0.12 per sampled candidate. The same Hint Bank also transfers to weaker backbones, including a 19.7 percentage-point pass-rate gain on Doubao-2.0-lite.
2606.12387.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）透過文本到 SQL 的方式實現了數據庫訪問的民主化，但從原型轉向生產環境仍然困難重重。實際部署必須處理嚴格的 SQL 方言、大型模式和不斷變化的用戶偏好，而有監督的微調成本高且僵化，代理的測試時間擴展則昂貴。我們提出了 Tahoe，一個將提示優化視為動態數據管理問題的系統。Tahoe 在開發和部署之間使用一個基於錯誤驅動的提示學習管道，將調試痕跡整合成結構化的提示庫。編譯器反饋被提煉成可重用的語法提示，用於特定方言的規則，而執行和用戶反饋則轉化為語義提示，用於特定模式和用戶的邏輯。Tahoe 進一步引入了一個策略層，將衝突的用戶意圖建模為在共享自然語言觸發下的競爭策略，並包含近期信號和後學習歸因統計，總結實證成功、損害、靜止和支持。在推理時，Tahoe 檢索相關提示並引導 LLM 通過邏輯規劃，然後進行 SQL 合成。我們實施並評估了開發階段的工作流程，將部署時的人類反饋更新留待未來工作。在 Spider 2.0-Snow 上，Tahoe 在不更新模型參數的情況下顯著改善了文本到 SQL 的性能。在使用 GPT-5.5 的 113 個有監督的 Spider 2.0-Snow-0212 範例中，Tahoe 將通過率從 61.95% 提高到 79.42%，將 pass-at-4 從 72.57% 提高到 87.61%，實現了 100% 的 Snowflake 語法通過率，並將每個樣本候選的平均編譯器反饋批評輪次從 2.79 減少到 0.12。相同的提示庫也可以轉移到較弱的骨幹上，包括在 Doubao-2.0-lite 上提高 19.7 個百分點的通過率。
2606.12387.author: Zhiyi Chen et.al.
2606.12387.authors: Zhiyi Chen, Jie Song, Peng Li
